Paper Title/Study: Inter-Relationship between Malaria, Probiotic Food Intake and Gut Microbiota Status among Malaria Patients
Authors: Awah-Nanzdum AJ, Metoh TN, Mbifung MK, Fru CT, Djouosseu AC, Yensii NG, Bongjo NB, Ngo-Matip M, Mbofung CM
Biomarkers: parasite density, gut microbiota (E. coli, Klebsiella, Pseudomonas, Enterococci faecalis, Proteus)
Study Population: 75 participants aged 2-18 years with confirmed malaria parasite density of 2000 trophozoites/uL.
Outcome / Finding: Significant change in gut bacteria community and reduced parasite burden.
Method: Analytical, clinical trial and longitudinal study
Contribution: Problem: To investigate the effect of orally co-administered Arthrospira platensis and Artemisinin-based Combination Therapy (ACT) on the human gut microbiota and malaria parasite burden.
Contribution: Result: The combination of artemether-lumefantrine and Arthrospira platensis significantly modified the gut bacteria community and reduced malaria parasite burden more rapidly than artemether-lumefantrine alone.

Paper Title/Study: Plasmodium berghei ANKA causes intestinal malaria associated with dysbiosis
Authors: Taniguchi T, Miyauchi E, Nakamura S, Hirai M, Suzue K, Imai T, Nomura T, Handa T, Okada H, Shimokawa C, Onishi R, Olia A, Hirata J, Tomita H, Ohno H, Horii T, Hisaeda H
Contribution: Problem: Investigating intestinal malaria associated with dysbiosis.
Contribution: Result: Plasmodium berghei ANKA causes intestinal malaria associated with dysbiosis.

Paper Title/Study: Gut microbiota elicits a protective immune response against malaria transmission
Authors: Yilmaz B, Portugal S, Tran TM, Gozzelino R, Ramos S, Gomes J, Regalado A, Cowan PJ, d'Apice AJ, Chong AS, Doumbo OK, Traore B, Crompton PD, Silveira H, Soares MP
Contribution: Problem: Investigating if gut microbiota elicits a protective immune response against malaria transmission.
Contribution: Result: Gut microbiota elicits a protective immune response against malaria transmission via anti-Alpha-Gal antibodies.
